messages.content: Build an Effect v3 Schema for a Product record with id:string, name:string, price:number and a decoder that takes unknown input and returns Either<ParseError, Product>.
What's the Effect v3 Schema way to decode unknown JSON into a typed Order record without throwing? Show a minimal example.
Build an Effect v3 Schema for a User record (id:string, age:number) and a total parser that returns User | string (string on failure, using the ParseError _tag).

Write an Effect v3 Schema module for a Booking record with nested fields (customer with name/email, slot with start/end ISO strings) and produce a total decoder over unknown. Constraints: strict types, no any, use Schema.Date for the slot times.
Add an optional description field to this Effect Schema and re-derive the decoder: const Item = Schema.Struct({ sku: Schema.String, qty: Schema.Number });

Refactor this hand-rolled parser to use Effect Schema: function parseEvent(u: unknown) { if (!u || typeof u !== 'object') throw new Error('not object'); const o = u as Record<string, unknown>; if (typeof o.id !== 'string') throw new Error('id'); if (typeof o.count !== 'number') throw new Error('count'); return { id: o.id, count: o.count }; }
Build an Effect v3 branded Email type using Schema. The brand should only accept strings that match a simple `x@y` pattern, and the decoder should fail on invalid input.
What's the Effect v3 way to combine Schema validation with a brand for positive integers? Show a minimal PositiveInt example.
Explain briefly why combining Schema.brand with Schema.Number is preferable to a manual type guard, then show a working typed UserId implementation.

Write an Effect v3 Schema module that defines a SKU brand (uppercase alphanumerics, 3–10 chars) and a Quantity brand (int between 1 and 999), then a decoder for a LineItem that composes both. Constraints: strict types, no any.
Add a brand to this Schema so only strings matching /^[A-F0-9]{6}$/ decode successfully, and expose the decoder: const Hex = Schema.String;
Build a typed Effect Schema for a Percent value (number between 0 and 100, one decimal place allowed) that returns Either<ParseError, Percent> where Percent is a branded number.
Build an fp-ts 2.x pipeline that parses an unknown input into Option<{name:string; age:number}>. Use O.fromNullable, O.chain, and a helper that type-guards records.
What's the fp-ts 2.x way to parse an unknown HTTP body into Either<string, {url:string; method:string}>? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly the difference between Option-style parsing and Either-style parsing of unknown input, then show a working fp-ts 2.x example that demonstrates when you want the error channel.
Write an fp-ts 2.x parser that takes unknown and returns Either<string, {port:number; host:string}>. Constraints: no any, the error channel is a single string, use pipe + E.chainW.
Build an fp-ts 2.x reader that extracts an optional `x:number` field from an unknown input and returns a number with a default of 0 on any miss. Use pipe + O.fromNullable + O.chain + O.getOrElse.
Build an Effect v3 Data.taggedEnum for an ApiResult with Success, NotFound, ServerError variants, and a pattern-matching function using Match.value / Match.tag that returns a user-facing string.
What's the Effect v3 way to define a RemoteData union (Idle / Loading / Loaded / Failed) using Data.taggedEnum and exhaustively match on it? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly why Data.taggedEnum with Match.exhaustive is safer than a manual switch on _tag, then show a typed PaymentEvent example with three cases.
Build an Effect v3 module that models a form state as Data.taggedEnum (Pristine / Editing / Submitting / Submitted) and exposes a reducer-style transition function matched via Match.value.
Add a new Cancelled variant to this taggedEnum and update the match: const { Queued, Running, Done } = Data.taggedEnum<{ Queued: {}; Running: {p:number}; Done: {out:string} }>();

Refactor this union of plain objects into a Data.taggedEnum with a Match.value consumer: type Msg = {_tag:'Hi'; name:string} | {_tag:'Bye'; reason:string}; function describe(m:Msg){ switch(m._tag){ case 'Hi': return 'hi '+m.name; case 'Bye': return 'bye '+m.reason; } }

Build an Effect v3 typed env loader that reads DATABASE_URL (string, required), PORT (int, default 3000), and DEBUG (bool, default false) via Effect.Config. Return a program that logs the resolved config.
What's the Effect v3 way to load a nested config object (server.host, server.port, logging.level)? Show a minimal example with Config.nested.
Explain briefly why Effect.Config is preferable to directly reading process.env, then show a working typed loader with two required and one defaulted value.
Write an Effect v3 Config loader for a retry policy (attempts:int, baseMs:int, maxMs:int) with defaults 3/100/5000. Constraints: strict types, no any, expose the result as Effect<RetryPolicy>.
Add a FEATURE_FLAGS comma-separated list to this Config: const cfg = Config.all({ name: Config.string('NAME') });
Build a typed fp-ts 2.x sort for an array of User records. Sort primarily by role priority (admin < editor < viewer) and secondarily by name ascending. Use N.Ord + Ord.contramap + A.sortBy.
What's the fp-ts 2.x way to derive an Eq for a Product by sku only, so A.uniq dedupes by sku? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly how Ord.contramap lets you lift a primitive Ord into a domain Ord, then show a working typed example that sorts an array of orders by their date field.

Write fp-ts 2.x Ord instances for sorting an Invoice list by status priority then amount descending. Constraints: strict types, no any, expose a single Ord<Invoice> built with Ord.contramap and Ord.reverse.
What's the fp-ts 2.x way to chain three sort keys — done ascending, priority ascending, createdAt ascending — over a Task list? Show a minimal example.
Fix this fp-ts 2.x Ord so it handles ties by falling back to a secondary comparator: const ord = pipe(N.Ord, Ord.contramap((u:User)=>u.age));
Build an Effect v3 example showing structural equality between two records via Data.struct and Equal.equals.
What's the Effect v3 way to get stable Hash codes and structural Equal for your own domain records? Show a minimal example.
Explain briefly why Equal.equals over Data.struct values is preferable to deep-equal libraries, then show a working typed example that uses the result as a HashSet key.
Write an Effect v3 module that represents a Money value with Data.struct, exposes an `equal` helper via Equal.equals, and a `hash` via Hash.hash. Constraints: strict types, no any.
Build an Effect v3 example that uses Data.array to make two nested arrays structurally equal via Equal.equals, where a plain array comparison would fail.
